name: ICNA Council for Social Justice
mission: To systematically facilitate assertive Muslim involvement in the field of human struggle for the rights of the poor and oppressed.
scoreSummary: Icna Council for Social Justice shows strong impact and good alignment, with zakat compliance.
zakatClaimEvidence: ICNA Council for Social Justice is listed as "Zakat eligible" on Feeling Blessed. Their website also features a "Zakat Calculator - ICNA CSJ" and mentions "Zakat Qualification". Furthermore, they explicitly discuss how zakat should be given in America to the poor. (Source: https://www.feelingblessed.org/icnacsj)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

components.evidence: Explicit zakat program (+4); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Islamic identity (+1); Asnaf: fuqara (+5)

components.improvement_suggestion: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.

components.evidence: Theory of change: BASIC (emerging org baseline)

all_citations.claim: The charity has a score of 86.5/100, a program expense ratio of 82.6%, and 7.3 months of working capital.
The organization is zakat-eligible and accepts donations for social justice causes.
The organization reported total revenue of $763,582 and fundraising efficiency of $0.10 per $1 raised.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization maintains a solid Charity Navigator score of 86.5/100, reflecting strong financial health and a high program expense ratio of 82.6% [1]. Its zakat eligibility and focus on religious and cultural advocacy create a high alignment for Muslim donors [2].
areas_for_improvement: Enhancing data transparency to improve overall data confidence scores
Expanding program documentation to provide more granular evidence of long-term impact

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization shows high alignment for donors seeking to support religious and cultural causes, specifically through its zakat-eligible programs [2]. Its mission to involve the Muslim community in human rights struggles addresses a high-urgency cause with a clear religious mandate [1][2].
dimension_explanations.impact: The charity demonstrates high financial efficiency with a program expense ratio of 82.6% and holds 7.3 months of working capital to ensure operational stability [1][3]. While it focuses on capacity building within the social justice sector, more public data is needed to reach the highest impact tiers [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: The ICNA Council for Social Justice empowers the Muslim community to advocate for the rights of the poor and oppressed through systematic social justice initiatives.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 82.6%, ensuring the majority of funds reach core initiatives
Strong fundraising efficiency with only $0.10 spent per $1 raised
Zakat-eligible status providing a verified pathway for religious giving
baseline_narrative.summary: The ICNA Council for Social Justice is a non-profit organization dedicated to facilitating assertive Muslim involvement in the struggle for human rights [1]. With a total revenue of $763,582, the organization operates as a zakat-eligible entity, allowing donors to fulfill their religious obligations while supporting social justice causes [2][3].

rich_narrative.headline: The ICNA Council for Social Justice empowers the Muslim community to advocate for the rights of the poor and oppressed through systematic social justice initiatives.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support systemic social justice and advocacy through a lean, zakat-eligible Muslim organization.
donor_motivations: Want to fulfill zakat through advocacy for the oppressed
Value high financial efficiency and low overhead
Prioritize long-term systemic change over immediate direct aid
Support assertive Muslim involvement in US civil rights
giving_considerations: Gifts support advocacy and capacity building rather than direct material aid
The organization is in a high-growth phase with a 22.7% CAGR
Impact is measured through policy influence and community empowerment
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking high-frequency direct service metrics (e.g., number of meals served) or those requiring RCT-level evidence.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects verified financial efficiency and documented mission, but lacks granular beneficiary-level impact data.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: ICNA CSJ facilitates Muslim involvement in human rights struggles to protect the poor and oppressed through advocacy and education.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that advocacy efforts translate into tangible policy changes or community protections.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a 22.7% growth rate and a small absolute budget, the org can absorb significant capital to expand advocacy.
strategic_priorities: Systematic Muslim involvement in human rights
Advocacy for the poor and oppressed

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by an exceptionally high <cite id="1">82.6% program expense ratio</cite> and strong revenue growth.

strengths.detail: The organization directs <cite id="1">82.6% of its expenses toward programs</cite>, significantly exceeding industry benchmarks for lean operations.
ICNA CSJ holds an <cite id="1">overall score of 86.86.5/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>, reflecting a solid commitment to transparency and governance.
The charity has achieved a <cite id="3">3-year revenue CAGR of 22.7%</cite>, indicating increasing donor trust and organizational scale.

strengths_deep_dive: ICNA CSJ exhibits high financial resilience, maintaining <cite id="1">7.3 months of working capital</cite> in working capital. This stability is paired with a governance structure where <cite id="1">100% of the board is independent</cite>, ensuring objective oversight of the mission. Furthermore, the organization maintains a <cite id="1">perfect Leadership score of 100</cite> from Charity Navigator, highlighting its strategic clarity and organizational health. Its zakat program is specifically structured to serve categories such as <cite id="2">the poor (fuqara) and the needy (masakin)</cite>, aligning religious compliance with professional advocacy standards.
rich_narrative.summary: The ICNA Council for Social Justice (ICNA CSJ) serves as a specialized advocacy and social justice arm for the American Muslim community, focusing on the rights of the poor and oppressed. The organization has demonstrated significant growth, reporting <cite id="3">$834,440 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 tax year, representing a <cite id="3">3-year revenue CAGR of 22.7%</cite>. ICNA CSJ operates with a high degree of financial focus on its mission, maintaining a <cite id="1">program expense ratio of 82.6%</cite> according to recent financial filings. This efficiency is further bolstered by <cite id="1">strong fundraising efficiency</cite>, spending only <cite id="1">$0.09 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions. Beyond its financial metrics, the organization is deeply rooted in Islamic principles of justice (Adl), providing a <cite id="2">verified zakat-eligible pathway</cite> for donors to support systemic advocacy. Its work encompasses a broad range of social justice issues, including civil rights, poverty alleviation, and human rights advocacy, ensuring that Muslim voices are represented in the broader struggle for equity in the United States.
